name: 5q35_NSD1_Sotos_BPs
description: 5q35.2-q35.3 region flanked by two LCR blocks (Sotos-REPs) at >99% identity. NAHR mediates the recurrent ~1.9 Mb microdeletion causing classical Sotos syndrome (NSD1 haploinsufficiency); reciprocal duplications cause a growth-failure phenotype. The 5q35 microdeletion is the most common pathogenic SD-mediated rearrangement on chr5q.

architecture.internal_structure: Two flanking Sotos-REPs of ~400 kb at >99% identity (proximal and distal); unique core contains NSD1, FGFR4, RAB24, MXD3. NSD1 belongs to NSD methyltransferase family (paralog NSD2/WHSC1 on 4p16, NSD3 on 8p11).
